items.title: Enhancement of a nuclear factor of activated T cells (NFAT) reporter for the study of G protein-coupled receptors
items.author: Graham Ladds
items.content: Cell-based assays are fundamental to G protein-coupled receptor (GPCRs) drug discovery. Here we report the enhancement of a Nuclear Factor of Activated T cells response element reporter for use in bi-allelic expression systems and primary cells.

items.title: Two distinct modes of Vgll4-mediated Tead regulation control organ size in zebrafish
items.author: Virginie Lecaudey
items.content: Genetic and functional analysis in zebrafish shows that Vgll4 limits tissue growth by inhibiting Yap1–Tead– mediated activation and promoting Tead-dependent repression, providing a framework for how Hippo pathway output regulates organ size in vivo.

items.title: Developmental trajectories of somatostatin-positive interneurons in the human auditory cortex from migration to maturation
items.author: Soumya Iyengar
items.content: Somatostatin-positive interneurons exhibit peak neurite complexity during fetal and postnatal stages followed by pruning in adolescence and a peak in density during childhood, suggesting potential roles in critical periods of auditory plasticity.

items.title: A broad-spectrum Chromobacterium biolarvicide formulation disrupts mosquito development through gut damage and apoptotic cell death and compromises vector competence
items.author: George Dimopoulos
items.content: Chromobacterium Csp_P guar gum-based pellets induce irreversible gut damage, apoptosis, and high larval mortality across mosquito vectors, causing developmental collapse, reduced adult fitness, and decreased malaria and dengue transmission potential.

items.title: Gram-negative-staining Bacillaceae with thick cell wall and monoderm architecture uncover evolutionary diversity and challenge Gram-based classification
items.author: Gabriela Olmedo-Álvarez
items.content: Genomic and microscopy evidence shows that the Bacillaceae, assumed to only stain Gram-positive, actually have some lineages staining Gram-negative, yet unexpectedly maintaining a thick wall and no outer membrane, challenging Gram staining assumptions.

items.title: Region- and cell type-specific changes in gene expression in the cerebellum after classical fear conditioning
items.author: Yong-Seok Lee
items.content: Cerebellar transcriptomic analyses reveal region- and cell type–specific gene expression changes during classical fear conditioning and memory retrieval, highlighting roles of deep cerebellar nuclei neurons and Kit+ inhibitory neurons in non-motor learning.

items.title: Deciphering the multi-site phos-code of IRBIT underlying its binding to IP<sub>3</sub>R
items.author: Katsuhiko Mikoshiba
items.content: The phosphorylation-dependent binding model of IRBIT and IP3R sheds light on understanding the mechanism of IP3/IRBIT/ IP3R interaction that exerts a significant signaling hub inside the cell.

items.title: E/I imbalance and internal noise cause weak neural representations and face recognition challenges in ASD
items.author: Lang Chen
items.content: The deep neural networks suggest that E/I imbalance could cause face recognition challenges and weak representations of faces, suggesting it as a potential neurobiological factor of neurocognitive and social challenges in ASD.
